paper_title: Effects of culture salinity on growth and reproduction of the polychaete Dendronereis chipolini

entities.id: Effects of culture salinity on growth and reproduction of the polychaete Dendronereis chipolini
entities.description: The main research problem investigating how different salinity levels affect the development of Dendronereis chipolini.
The primary field of research concerning the farming of aquatic organisms.

entities.id: College of Aquaculture and Fisheries (CAF), Can Tho University
entities.description: Academic institution in Viet Nam where the research was conducted.
Experimental design involving four different salinity levels to test the polychaete's performance.
The experimental environment where the young worms were stocked.
The material used to feed the worms during the 150-day experiment.
The statistical tool used for data analysis.
Statistical method used to identify differences among treatments.
The theoretical goal of developing a protocol to produce feed for shrimp broodstock.
The optimal salinity level identified for the maximum growth rate.
The measurement of survival at the end of the experiment for the 20 ppt treatment.
The recorded reproductive output at 25 ppt salinity.
The average length achieved by worms in the 20 ppt treatment.
The rate of weight increase per day in the 20 ppt treatment.
The rate of mature worms recorded in the 20 ppt treatment.
Water quality monitoring value during the experiment.
